articles.title: Iran's Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf Confirms That Frozen Assets To Be Released Soon | Trump | News18
articles.description: Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf has confirmed that the process to release Iran’s frozen assets could begin soon, signaling a potential breakthrough amid ongoing diplomatic efforts between Tehran and Washington. The development comes as discussions around sanctions relief, regional stability, and the future of US-Iran relations continue to draw global attention. articles.title: Release Of Frozen Assets, Nuclear Inspections: Why US And Iran Differ On Switzerland Talks
articles.description: After Switzerland talks, JD Vance said that Iran had agreed to allow inspectors from the IAEA back into the country, which was rejected by Tehran.

articles.title: US Iran War Live Updates: Iran says Strait of Hormuz to be administered by Tehran after talks with US
articles.description: Iran War News Live: Iran's chief negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said the Strait of Hormuz will be administered by Tehran under international law following talks in Switzerland aimed at ending the US-Israeli conflict with Iran. Ghalibaf said discussions yielded progress on the strait, Lebanon, oil sanctions waivers and the release of frozen Iranian funds.

articles.title: Hajj 2027: How to apply online, eligibility, quota and key details explained
articles.description: The Centre has announced the Hajj Policy 2027 and opened applications online. Pilgrims can register through the Hajj Committee portal and the Hajj Suvidha App.

articles.description: Trump warned that the US would take action if Iran fails to honour its commitments, arguing that preventing Tehran from acquiring a nuclear weapon outweighs concerns about economic fallout.

articles.description: Former White House Chief Medical Advisor Dr Anthony Fauci has been subpoenaed to testify publicly before the Senate Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs Committee, according to Senator Rand Paul.

articles.description: Andy Burnham’s return to Westminster begins a new phase in his political journey after years in regional leadership. His re-entry has raised questions about his long-term ambitions within Labour’s national politics.

articles.title: Ludhiana: Police vehicle vandalised during stir outside CP office
articles.description: The protesters, most of them dressed in Nihang attire, had gathered outside the CP office to submit a complaint against a Ludhiana-based social media influencer accused of posting content that hurt Sikh religious sentiments

articles.title: Congress's Tariq Anwar says BJP running 'livestock market' for MPs
articles.description: Congress leader Tariq Anwar likened political realignments to a 'livestock market,' accusing the BJP of horse-trading. The remarks came after six Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs joined the Eknath Shinde-led faction, prompting harsh criticism from the opposition.

articles.title: Jaishankar holds high-level talks in Mongolia to expand cooperation
articles.description: EAM S Jaishankar visited Mongolia, holding high-level talks with the President, Speaker, and FM to expand strategic and economic cooperation. Discussions focused on development projects, mining, energy, and strengthening people-centric partnerships.

articles.title: Atishi: BJP diverting from power cuts with DISCOM audit allegations
articles.description: Delhi LoP Atishi accused the BJP of using DISCOM audit allegations to divert attention from power cuts. She asserted AAP ordered the audit, while BJP Minister Ashish Sood pointed to the DISCOMs' court challenge as proof of an AAP nexus.

articles.title: 'Checked Trump's SM before coming here’: Ambassador Huckabee's quip leaves room in stitches
articles.description: US Ambassador to Israel Mike Huckabee joked on Sunday that he checked President Donald Trump's social media accounts before delivering a speech in Jerusalem to ensure he had not been dismissed overnight.Speaking at the JNS International Policy Summit, Huckabee took a light-hearted swipe at Trump's tendency to announce personnel decisions through social media posts.'So today, before I got here, I did something that I thought was very important. I checked President Trump's social media to make sure this was not my last speech in Israel,' Huckabee said.

articles.title: Rare 4-sided pillar with Buddha Parinirvana sculpture found in Adilabad

articles.title: Tibetan leaders urge India, BRICS to raise Tibet issue with China
articles.description: Tibetan leaders in exile have urged India and BRICS countries to discuss the Tibet issue during the BRICS NSA meeting, stating that the India-China border dispute cannot be resolved until the matter of Tibet's freedom is addressed.

articles.title: Jaishankar in Mongolia to boost strategic and spiritual partnership ties
articles.description: EAM S Jaishankar arrived in Mongolia for a high-level visit to strengthen strategic and spiritual ties. He met President Khurelsukh Ukhnaa and held talks with his counterpart on boosting economic cooperation in mining, energy, and agriculture.

articles.title: Bidadi row: DK Shivakumar dares JDS to debate issue in Assembly
articles.description: Karnataka CM DK Shivakumar challenged the JDS to raise the Bidadi Township issue in the Assembly on behalf of HD Kumaraswamy. Shivakumar noted that Kumaraswamy himself initiated the project, which is now being opposed by JDS and BJP.

articles.title: Uddhav Thackeray to respond soon after 6 MPs join Eknath Shinde's camp
articles.description: Uddhav Thackeray stated he will respond to the defection of six Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs to the rival faction led by Eknath Shinde. Shinde welcomed the MPs, calling the move a continuation of the 2022 split aimed at saving the original party.

articles.title: Landowners cannot be allowed to stall projects of nationwide priority: Madras High Court
articles.description: Madras High Court rules landowners cannot impede nationally important projects, emphasizing public good over individual interests in land acquisition.

articles.description: The move allows nations importing Iranian oil and other energy products to maintain transactions for the next two months under the terms of the license.

articles.title: 'HOPE U.S. IS SERIOUS': Iranian Official Makes BIG Announcement On Swiss Peace Talks | WATCH
articles.description: Iran on Monday announced that a major progress had been made in the ongoing negotiations with the United States, highlighting new agreements aimed at stabilizing the situation in Lebanon and ensuring maritime security in the Strait of Hormuz. Speaking about the latest round of talks, Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmail Baghaei said a new mechanism known as the “De-confliction Cell” has been established with the involvement of mediators to monitor and preserve the Lebanon ceasefire. The Iranian official said a new mechanism known as the “De-confliction Cell” has been established with the involvement of mediators to monitor and preserve the Lebanon ceasefire. He also announced that an agreement had been reached on a mechanism to guarantee the safe passage of ships through the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's most strategically important waterways.

articles.title: Roving Periscope: British PM Starmer quits; successor likely by September
articles.description: Virendra Pandit New Delhi: Europe—particularly the United Kingdom—laughed derisively at India’s political instability in the 1977-1999 period. On Monday, Sir Keir Starmer became the sixth British Prime Minister to quit in 10 years! Following dissidence in his party, he announced to quit, setting out a timetable to step down from Downing Street in the [...]

articles.title: “At 52, I left my husband; what my daughter revealed next left me...": How a mother finally broke free from years of abuse and chose peace over pain
articles.description: After enduring nearly two decades of abuse, Baljinder Kaur, at 52, finally left a marriage that had caused immense suffering. Driven by a desire to protect her daughter, she remarried at 33, only to face years of emotional and physical mistreatment. A heartbreaking revelation about her daughter's abuse became her breaking point.

articles.title: Just In: Sena Rebel MPs Meet Eknath Shinde at His Residence Amid Political Buzz
articles.description: Rebel Shiv Sena MPs are currently meeting Maharashtra Chief Minister Eknath Shinde at his residence, triggering fresh political speculation. The high-profile meeting comes amid ongoing tensions within the Shiv Sena (UBT) faction and reports of internal realignments. Further details on the outcome of the meeting are awaited. n18oc_politics n18oc_breaking-news

articles.title: 'They abuse and harass men...': Viral video claims three women tore their own clothes after a heated argument
articles.description: A viral video on X has sparked a heated debate about accountability and evidence after showing a public confrontation between three women and a man. The uploader alleged the women were abusing boys and then tore their own clothes when confronted. Many users are emphasizing the importance of video evidence in such disputes, with some criticizing perceived misuse of victimhood and laws.

articles.title: 'Hunter has been hunted': Rohit Pawar takes dig at Eknath Shinde over Nashik MLC poll results
articles.description: Following the Mahayuti alliance's defeat in the Nashik MLC elections, NCP (SP) MLA Rohit Pawar criticized Deputy CM Eknath Shinde, stating the "hunter 'Tiger' got hunted." He announced a farmer agitation on June 29, citing government distrust. Meanwhile, Shiv Sena leader Deepak Kesarkar suggested potential Shiv Sena (UBT) MP defections would strengthen the NDA, while Aaditya Thackeray accused the BJP of attempting to alter the Constitution by engineering these shifts.

articles.title: Entire political science of data doctoring: Congress slams government on rural wage figures
articles.description: Congress: Congress has accused the Modi government of inflating rural wage growth through changes in data collection methods. Jairam Ramesh claimed revised sampling distorted wage trends, reigniting debate over jobs, consumption, economic slowdown and official statistics.

articles.title: Former East Timor president, independence fighter Francisco Guterres dies at 71
Is Bharat Forge The Next Multibagger? Defence Sector Outlook Explained By Gaurav Sharma
articles.description: India's defence sector remains a key area of interest for investors as rising geopolitical tensions and increasing focus on indigenous manufacturing continue to support growth prospects. Gaurav Sharma, Associate VP & Head of Research, Globe Capital, said that Bharat Forge remains a fundamentally strong company with a solid track record, improving defence order inflows and stable financial performance. He noted that the company has been steadily expanding its presence in niche defence segments and remains a positive long-term bet despite broader market volatility. Sharma also highlighted that defence is a multi-year growth story driven by global security concerns and higher spending on military capabilities. Among his preferred picks in the sector are HAL, Bharat Electronics (BEL) and Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders.
